what_it_does: Defines a React settings canvas for configuring OpenAI access, testing connectivity, fetching and selecting image models, setting model-specific image defaults, toggling image generation behavior, and clearing local studio storage.
observations: Uses React state and effects via destructured global React aliases.
Reads and writes OpenAI settings through window.cvOpenAI.
Subscribes to settings and model-cache updates on mount.
Auto-refreshes the image model list on first mount when configured and no live cache exists.
Stores pending credential input separately from persisted settings until Save credentials is clicked.
Shows a connection banner, credential form, model picker, capability-aware defaults, feature toggles, and local storage meter.
Uses browser globals such as confirm, location.reload, and window.dsaToast.
Model cards expose capability badges for generation, editing, variations, style, and multi-image output.
standouts: Combines credential management, model discovery, image defaults, and local storage controls in one settings canvas.
Model defaults are derived from selected model capabilities instead of fixed option lists.
The model picker groups models by declared tier and renders capability metadata per model.
conventions: Attaches the exported component to window.Settings rather than using module exports.
Uses global application services and components through window and global identifiers.
Uses className-based styling with several inline style objects for local layout tweaks.
Keeps helper components and formatter functions in the same file as the Settings canvas.
Uses optional chaining around model-cache APIs and image-store APIs.
concerns: The two subscribe effects do not return unsubscribe cleanup functions, which may leave listeners active after unmount if the subscription API expects cleanup.
pendingKey, pendingProxy, and pendingOrg initialize from settings but are not synchronized when settings later change through the subscription.
ModelDefaults accepts a models prop but does not use it.
The selected model capabilities are read from window.cvOpenAI rather than from the models prop rendered by ModelPicker, so display data and default controls can diverge if the sources differ.
Several global dependencies are assumed to exist without local guards, including CanvasHeader, CvIcon, ViShape, and window.cvOpenAI.
notes: This file is the OpenAI-focused settings surface for image generation configuration and local studio storage control.
questions: Does window.cvOpenAI.subscribe return an unsubscribe function that should be returned from useEffect?
Should pending credential fields update when settings change externally?
Should ModelDefaults use the passed models list or remove the unused prop?
What quota is intended by the hard-coded 5 MB storage meter?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: the file focuses on system-wide OpenAI settings for image generation and related local storage controls.
intention_signals: The file seems intended to make OpenAI image features self-service inside the studio by guiding credential setup, validating access, exposing available image models, and adapting defaults to the selected model.
novelty: A live model picker with tier grouping and capability-aware defaults is distinct within this file.
contribution: Provides a settings substrate for connecting an image-capable OpenAI service, selecting live-discovered image models, configuring generation defaults, enabling chat-driven image generation, applying a safe-mode toggle, and managing locally stored studio imagery/state.
summary_for_embedding: React settings canvas for OpenAI image-generation credentials, connection testing, live image model selection, model-aware defaults, feature toggles, and browser-local storage clearing.
intention_for_embedding: Built to activate and manage OpenAI-backed image generation across the studio from a single local settings surface.

symbols.does: Renders the full settings canvas and coordinates OpenAI settings, model cache state, connection testing, and storage controls.
symbols.description: Settings initializes local state from window.cvOpenAI, subscribes to settings and model payload updates, refreshes model data when configured, and renders the page sections. It owns credential draft state, model refresh state, test result state, and local storage display values.

symbols.does: Fetches the live image model list when OpenAI is configured and records errors or loading state.
symbols.description: refreshModels blocks with a user-facing error when no API key is configured, then calls window.cvOpenAI.listImageModels with force enabled. On success it updates modelPayload and shows a toast with the number of image models; on failure it stores the error message.

symbols.does: Applies a partial settings update through window.cvOpenAI and mirrors the returned settings locally.
symbols.description: patch is the file-local settings mutation helper. It delegates persistence and merging to window.cvOpenAI.updateSettings, then updates the Settings component state with the returned value.

symbols.does: Persists trimmed credential fields and clears any previous connection-test result.
symbols.description: saveCredentials writes apiKey, proxyUrl, and organization from the pending form fields through patch. It also emits a credentials-saved toast and resets testResult so stale connection feedback is removed after saving.

symbols.does: Runs an OpenAI connection test and displays success or failure feedback.
symbols.description: runTest sets a testing state, calls window.cvOpenAI.testConnection, and stores a message containing visible model counts on success. Because the test refreshes the model cache as a side effect, it also reloads modelPayload from window.cvOpenAI.getCachedModels afterward.

symbols.does: Computes the displayed API key string based on whether reveal mode is active.
symbols.description: masked returns an empty string for no key, the raw key when reveal is true, a full bullet mask for short keys, or a partial prefix/suffix mask for longer keys. It is used in the credential hint to show the active key without exposing the full value by default.

symbols.does: Renders selectable image model cards grouped by tier.
symbols.description: ModelPicker shows an empty state when no models are loaded, otherwise groups models by their tier field and renders tier headers plus cards. Each card displays model id, release, tagline, capability badges, size count, quality count, and selected-state marker.

symbols.does: Renders default image-generation controls based on the selected model capabilities.
symbols.description: ModelDefaults asks window.cvOpenAI for capabilities for settings.imageModel, then renders size, quality, style, and per-call variant controls or static text according to those capabilities. It patches settings when size, quality, or style values change.

symbols.does: Formats a timestamp as a short relative age string.
symbols.description: formatRelative returns never for missing timestamps, just now for sub-minute age, then minute, hour, or day counts based on Date.now. It is used to describe when the model list was pulled.
